edges.description: GATOR1 loss disinhibits mTORC1 signaling, increasing risk of focal cortical network hyperexcitability.
edges.source: GATOR1 Complex Loss and mTORC1 Disinhibition
edges.target: Focal Seizures With Variable Cortical Foci

edges.description: DEPDC5/GATOR1 loss is associated with cortical dysplasia lesions in a subset of patients.

nodes.description: Management generally begins with antiseizure medications used for focal epilepsy. Response is variable; some patients become almost seizure-free, while lesion-associated DEPDC5/GATOR1 cases can be drug resistant.

evidence.reference_title: Efficacy and safety of add-on antiseizure medications for focal epilepsy: A network meta-analysis.
evidence.snippet: All approved ASMs were effective as add-on treatment for focal epilepsy.

evidence.reference_title: The clinical features of familial focal epilepsy with variable foci and NPRL3 gene variant.
evidence.snippet: different therapeutic effects (refractory epilepsy/almost seizure free)

evidence.reference_title: Mutations in DEPDC5 cause familial focal epilepsy with variable foci.
evidence.snippet: Using exome sequencing, we detected DEPDC5 mutations in two affected families. We subsequently identified mutations in five of six additional published large families with FFEVF.

evidence.reference_title: A novel variation in DEPDC5 causing familial focal epilepsy with variable foci.
evidence.snippet: The results suggest that c.1217 + 2T>A variations in DEPDC5 might be the genetic etiology for FFEVF in this pedigree.

nodes.description: In some DEPDC5-positive families, germline and brain somatic DEPDC5 variants are associated with focal cortical dysplasia, consistent with a two-hit mTORopathy model for cortical lesions and drug-resistant focal epilepsy.

evidence.reference_title: Familial focal epilepsy with focal cortical dysplasia due to DEPDC5 mutations
evidence.snippet: Germline, germline mosaic, and brain somatic DEPDC5 mutations may cause epilepsy associated with FCD, reinforcing the link between mTORC1 pathway and FCDs.

evidence.reference_title: Familial focal epilepsy with focal cortical dysplasia due to DEPDC5 mutations
evidence.snippet: A brain somatic DEPDC5 mutation was identified in 1 patient in addition to the germline mutation.

evidence.reference_title: Deep histopathology genotype-phenotype analysis of focal cortical dysplasia type II differentiates between the GATOR1-altered autophagocytic subtype IIa and MTOR-altered migration deficient subtype IIb
evidence.snippet: Ten individuals carried loss-of-function variants in the GATOR1 complex encoding genes DEPDC5 (n = 7) and NPRL3 (n = 3), or gain-of-function variants in MTOR (n = 7).

nodes.description: Focal resection can be considered for drug-resistant focal epilepsy when a seizure focus or focal cortical dysplasia is identified, including DEPDC5-positive cases that have subtle or unremarkable MRI.
nodes.id: Epilepsy Surgery for Drug-Resistant DEPDC5/FCD Disease

evidence.reference_title: Familial focal epilepsy with focal cortical dysplasia due to DEPDC5 mutations
evidence.snippet: Three patients were seizure‐free postsurgically, and 1 had a worthwhile improvement.

evidence.reference_title: Seizure Control Outcomes following Resection of Cortical Dysplasia in Patients with DEPDC5 Variants: A Systematic Review and Individual Patient Data Analysis.
evidence.snippet: Thirty-seven of the 40 patients (37/40, 92.5%) with reported seizure frequency results had improvement.

nodes.description: Preclinical mouse-model evidence supports mTORC1 inhibition as a rational therapeutic strategy for DEPDC5-related epilepsy. Rapamycin, also known as sirolimus, prolonged survival and partially rescued behavioral hyperactivity in neuronal-specific Depdc5 knockout mice; this is experimental rather than established clinical care for FFEVF.
Focal cortical dysplasia can occur in DEPDC5/GATOR1-positive focal epilepsy, sometimes despite normal or atypical MRI, and contributes to drug-resistant focal seizures.

evidence.reference_title: Familial focal epilepsy with focal cortical dysplasia due to DEPDC5 mutations
evidence.snippet: All patients had drug‐resistant focal epilepsy, 5 of them underwent surgery, and 1 had a brain biopsy.

evidence.reference_title: Deep histopathology genotype-phenotype analysis of focal cortical dysplasia type II differentiates between the GATOR1-altered autophagocytic subtype IIa and MTOR-altered migration deficient subtype IIb
evidence.snippet: Whereas individuals with GATOR1 variants only presented with FCDIIa, i.e., lack of balloon cells, individuals with MTOR variants presented with both histopathology subtypes, FCDIIa and FCDIIb.

nodes.description: Affected relatives have focal-onset seizures, but the anatomical focus can differ between family members, including temporal, frontal, parietal, or occipital regions.
nodes.id: Focal Seizures With Variable Cortical Foci

evidence.reference_title: Familial partial epilepsy with variable foci: clinical features and linkage to chromosome 22q12.
evidence.snippet: Familial partial epilepsy with variable foci (FPEVF) is an autosomal dominant syndrome characterized by partial seizures originating from different brain regions in different family members in the absence of detectable structural abnormalities.

evidence.reference_title: Mutations in DEPDC5 cause familial focal epilepsy with variable foci.
evidence.snippet: Autosomal dominant familial focal epilepsy with variable foci (FFEVF) is notable because family members have seizures originating from different cortical regions.

nodes.description: Loss-of-function variants in DEPDC5, NPRL2, or NPRL3 impair GATOR1 complex regulation of mTORC1. The resulting excess mTORC1 signal transduction is a plausible upstream mechanism for altered cortical development, neuronal excitability, and focal seizure generation.
nodes.id: GATOR1 Complex Loss and mTORC1 Disinhibition

evidence.reference_title: GATOR1 Mutations Impair PI3 Kinase-Dependent Growth Factor Signaling Regulation of mTORC1
evidence.snippet: GATOR1 (GAP Activity TOward Rag 1) is an evolutionarily conserved GTPase-activating protein complex that controls the activity of mTORC1 (mammalian Target Of Rapamycin Complex 1) in response to amino acid availability in cells.

evidence.reference_title: GATOR1 Mutations Impair PI3 Kinase-Dependent Growth Factor Signaling Regulation of mTORC1
evidence.snippet: NPRL2-L105P is a loss-of-function mutation that disrupts protein interactions with NPRL3 and DEPDC5, impairing GATOR1 complex assembly and resulting in high mTORC1 activity even under conditions of amino acid deprivation.

evidence.reference_title: A Novel Loss-of-Function Mutation in the NPRL3 Gene Identified in Chinese Familial Focal Epilepsy with Variable Foci.
evidence.snippet: In addition, the expression of downstream molecular Phospho-p70 S6 kinase (P-s6k) are increased consequently.

evidence.reference_title: Chronic mTORC1 inhibition rescues behavioral and biochemical deficits resulting from neuronal Depdc5 loss in mice.
evidence.snippet: Depdc5flox/flox-Syn1Cre (Depdc5cc+) neuronal-specific Depdc5 knockout mice exhibit seizures and neuronal mTORC1 hyperactivation.

evidence.reference_title: A splicing variation in NPRL2 causing familial focal epilepsy with variable foci: additional cases and literature review
evidence.snippet: Here, we describe a variant, 339+2T>C, inNPRL2identified by trio whole-exome sequencing (WES) in a family.

evidence.reference_title: A splicing variation in NPRL2 causing familial focal epilepsy with variable foci: additional cases and literature review
evidence.snippet: This splicing variant that occurred at the 5′ end of exon 3 was confirmed by minigene assays, which affected alternative splicing and led to exon 3 skipping inNPRL2.

evidence.reference_title: A Novel Loss-of-Function Mutation in the NPRL3 Gene Identified in Chinese Familial Focal Epilepsy with Variable Foci.
evidence.snippet: Whole exome sequencing confirms a novel pathogenic mutation in the NPRL3 gene (c316C>T; p. Q106*).

evidence.reference_title: Identification of two rare NPRL3 variants in two Chinese families with familial focal epilepsy with variable foci 3: NGS analysis with literature review.
evidence.snippet: In family E1, the trio-WES analysis of the proband and her brother without apparent structural brain abnormalities identified a heterozygous variant in the NPRL3 gene (c.954C>A, p.Y318*, NM_001077350.3).
